title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Driven by rage, Patrick Redfern attacks Hercule Poirot by attempting to strangle him. Following this outburst, the narrative reflects on the nature of evil and the true roles played by the characters involved in the crime. While figures like Reverend Lane viewed Elena Marshall as a source of evil, the narrator argues that Elena was merely a victim of her own perceived vulnerabilities and the predatory designs of men. Her husband, Kenneth Marshall, had mistakenly attempted to protect her, despite her history of being targeted for exploitation.

The narrator identifies Patrick Redfern as the true source of evil, characterizing him as a predator who manipulates women for financial gain. Redfern likely deceived Elena by promising fraudulent investment opportunities, a tactic similar to how he had previously manipulated a woman named Corey. He was assisted by his wife, Christine Redfern, an actress who maintained a facade of fragility and intellectualism to conceal her true nature as an energetic former physical education teacher.

The murder was a meticulously timed operation. Redfern and Christine staged fake arguments to create a sense of domestic tension. On the day of the crime, Redfern prepared by unlocking a patio door to suggest an early swim and hiding a green Chinese-style hat to implicate Elena. He also arranged a secret meeting with Elena at Elfin Bay, instructing her to hide in a "fairy hole" if she sensed anyone approaching.
